earlyInitiativeExecution: No approved short-point examples in the current 20-point library.
sustainedPressureExecution: Neutralizes serve pressure, sustains depth and precision, uses safe targets, changes geometry with drop shots, and finishes after the point has been fully constructed.

earlyInitiativeExecution: Uses serve-created openings or return + 2 opportunities to move forward and finish without forcing the first ball.
sustainedPressureExecution: Builds with heavy margin, depth, shape, and direction; stretches opponents until the open court or forced error appears.

earlyInitiativeExecution: Uses the serve to create immediate first-ball control, steps forward quickly, and finishes with a backhand, smash, or other open-court strike.
sustainedPressureExecution: Absorbs serve pace, takes time away with early contact, and changes direction only after establishing control.

earlyInitiativeExecution: Recognizes serve/return-created openings early, moves inside the court, closes forward, and uses forehand, passing-shot, or drop-volley finishes.
sustainedPressureExecution: Can first neutralize a strong serve and extend the exchange before changing court position and finishing at the forecourt.

earlyInitiativeExecution: No approved short-point examples in the current 20-point library.
sustainedPressureExecution: Uses pace, depth, margin, and stable rally tolerance; waits for the short ball or directional opening, then accelerates or moves forward to finish.
